_comment: IAM Permission Boundary — Applied to all roles in workload accounts
_purpose: Sets the maximum permissions any role in a workload account can have. Even if a role is granted AdministratorAccess via an inline policy, the permission boundary caps what the role can actually do. This closes the IAM privilege escalation path.
_threat_closed: IAM privilege escalation within an account (creating a new role with elevated permissions), accidental over-permissioning, insider threat creating backdoor roles.
_design_note: This boundary is enforced via a Terraform module that attaches it during role creation. No workload role can be created without it. The boundary itself cannot be removed by roles subject to it — only by the management account via Organizations.
_key_denies: All IAM write actions — prevents role/policy creation to escalate privileges
All Organizations actions — prevents account manipulation
CloudTrail modification — already covered by SCP but defense-in-depth
Security Hub disable — preserves centralized findings
